title: Conditionally executed code should be reachable
description: Conditionally executed code should be reachableAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugcweunusedsuspiciouspitfallsymbolic-executionWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoConditional expressions which are alwaystrueorfalsecan lead tounreachable code.In the case below, the call ofDispose()never happens.var a = false; if (a) { Dispose(); // Never reached }ExceptionsThis rule will not raise an issue in either of these cases:When the condition is a singleconst boolconst bool debug = false; //... if (debug) { // Print something }When the condition is the literaltrueorfalse.In these cases, it is obvious the code is as intended.Available In:Detect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

detection_conditions: If statement with literal boolean condition (true or false)
Variable initialized to false and used in if condition without reassignment
triggering_conditions: if (true) followed by else block - else is unreachable
var a = false; if (a) - if block unreachable due to always-false variable
exclusion_conditions: const bool variable used in condition (intentional compile-time decision)
Literal true or false in condition (obvious intent)
Boolean expressions involving method calls or properties
never_trigger_on: const bool debug = false; if (debug) - excluded pattern
if (true) or if (false) without const - obvious intent
checks.reasoning: This rule detects unreachable code caused by static boolean conditions. The check_conditionally_executed_unreachable_code function identifies if (true) with else blocks, if (false) statements, and variables initialized to false used directly in conditions. It excludes const bool declarations and literal true/false as these are intentional. The rule helps prevent bugs from conditions that can never change their outcome.

validation_examples.reason: Else block is unreachable because condition is always true
If block unreachable because 'a' is always false

validation_examples.code: const bool debug = false; if (debug) { Print(); }
validation_examples.reason: Const bool is excluded pattern - intentional compile-time decision
